_seed_meta.description: Transforms Federal Award Notices and Agreements into actionable compliance frameworks. Extracts administrative requirements, financial obligations, and reporting requirements for post-award teams.

items.name: Award Notice/Agreement Compliance Analysis 1.0
items.description: Extract and organize all compliance requirements, terms, conditions, and administrative obligations from Award Notices

data.prompt:
You are analyzing a Federal Award Notice/Agreement document. Extract ALL terms, conditions, and compliance requirements to create a comprehensive post-award compliance framework.
Thoroughly search the entire document, including attachments, appendices, and referenced documents. Pay special attention to sections titled:

"Terms and Conditions"
"Special Terms and Conditions"
"Administrative Requirements"
"Programmatic Requirements"
"High Risk Terms"
"Award Specific Requirements"
"Federal Requirements"
"Regulatory Requirements"

Create a comprehensive response with these sections:

STANDARD FEDERAL REQUIREMENTS:

Uniform Guidance (2 CFR 200) applicability
Research Terms and Conditions (RTCs) applicability
Agency-specific regulations referenced
Statutory requirements mentioned


ADMINISTRATIVE TERMS & CONDITIONS:

Financial reporting requirements (frequency, deadlines, systems)
Progress reporting requirements (type, frequency, format)
Prior approval requirements (categorized by type)
Budget modification restrictions
Audit and record-keeping requirements
Property management requirements


PROGRAMMATIC TERMS & CONDITIONS:

Substantial involvement requirements (if cooperative agreement)
Technical reporting specifications
Deliverable requirements and deadlines
Performance metrics and measurement requirements
Collaboration/partnership obligations


HIGH-RISK OR SPECIAL CONDITIONS:

Enhanced monitoring requirements
Additional reporting obligations
Specific restrictions or limitations
Special approval processes
Risk mitigation measures


COMPLIANCE CALENDAR:
Create a table with:

Requirement Type
Specific Deadline/Frequency
Responsible Party
Consequences of Non-compliance



Be thorough. Search for keywords: 'condition,' 'requirement,' 'must,' 'shall,' 'comply,' 'report,' 'submit,' 'approve,' 'prior,' 'deadline,' 'CFR,' 'federal,' 'audit,' 'monitor.'
If information is not found for any section, explicitly state 'Not specified in the document' rather than omitting the section.

You are analyzing a Federal Award Notice/Agreement document. Extract ALL financial management requirements, budget details, and fiscal compliance obligations to establish proper financial oversight.
Thoroughly search the entire document, including budget attachments and financial terms. Pay special attention to sections titled:

"Award Amount"
"Budget Information"
"Financial Management"
"Cost Sharing/Matching"
"Indirect Costs"
"Financial Reporting"
"Payment/Drawdown Information"

Create a comprehensive response with these sections:

AWARD FINANCIAL DETAILS:

Total Award Amount
Budget Period Amounts (by year)
Federal Share vs. Non-Federal Share
Cost Sharing/Matching Requirements (amount and type)
Indirect Cost Rate and Base
Period of Performance dates


BUDGET CATEGORIES & RESTRICTIONS:
Create a table with:

Budget Category (Personnel, Equipment, etc.)
Approved Amount
Specific Restrictions or Limitations
Prior Approval Requirements


FINANCIAL REPORTING REQUIREMENTS:

Federal Financial Report (FFR) frequency and deadlines
Payment Management System (PMS) requirements
Cash management and drawdown procedures
Financial monitoring and oversight requirements


ALLOWABLE COSTS FRAMEWORK:

Reference to applicable cost principles (2 CFR 200, etc.)
Specific allowable cost guidance
Unallowable costs explicitly mentioned
Equipment purchase approval requirements


FINANCIAL COMPLIANCE OBLIGATIONS:

Audit requirements (Single Audit thresholds)
Record retention requirements
Financial monitoring and internal controls
Cost allocation and documentation standards



Be thorough. Search for keywords: 'budget,' 'cost,' 'financial,' 'funding,' 'allowable,' 'report,' 'FFR,' 'PMS,' 'drawdown,' 'match,' 'share,' 'indirect,' 'audit,' 'record.'
If information is not found for any section, explicitly state 'Not specified in the document' rather than omitting the section.

Role: You are an expert AI assistant specializing in organizing complex grant award information. Your primary function is to synthesize and reformat raw data extractions into a single, professional, and easily digestible summary document for grant managers and researchers.

Goal: Your task is to process the two provided raw text extractions—one on compliance and one on financial management—and merge them into a single, cohesive summary. The final output should be well-structured, free of redundancy, and easy to read.

Key Instructions:
1.  Synthesize and De-duplicate: Combine all information from both text inputs. If the same fact (e.g., award period) appears in both, present it only once in the appropriate section of the final summary.
2.  Preserve All Facts: Accurately transfer all specific data points, including dollar amounts, dates, award numbers, agency names, and referenced regulations (e.g., 2 CFR 200, PAPPG).
3.  Maintain Clarity on Missing Information: If the source text explicitly states that a detail is "Not specified in the document," retain that note. Do not infer or invent missing details.
4.  Use Professional Formatting: Employ Markdown formatting, including bold text for emphasis, tables for structured data, and horizontal rules to separate sections, as specified in the structure below.

---

### Desired Output Structure

Please format the combined information *exactly* according to the following template.

### **NSF Award Compliance & Financial Overview**

This document provides a consolidated summary of the compliance framework and financial management requirements for the specified National Science Foundation (NSF) award.

* **Project Title:** [Extract Project Title]
* **Awardee:** [Extract Awardee Name]
* **NSF Award Number (FAIN):** [Extract FAIN]
* **Period of Performance:** [Extract Start Date] – [Extract End Date]

---

### **Financial Summary**

| Financial Item                  | Details                                        |
| :------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------- |
| **Total Intended Award Amount** | [Extract Amount]                               |
| **Amount Obligated to Date** | [Extract Amount]                               |
| **Cost Sharing / Matching** | [Extract Requirement, e.g., "$0 (Not required)"] |
| **Indirect Cost (F&A) Rate** | [Extract Rate and Base, e.g., "FY22: 48.5% MTDC"] |

---

### **Approved Budget & Restrictions**

| Budget Category         | Approved Amount | Specific Restrictions & Notes                                                                          |
| :---------------------- | :-------------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [Populate all budget categories listed in the source, e.g., Postdoctoral Personnel, Domestic Travel, etc. If an amount is $0, state it clearly.] | [Extract $$]    | [Combine all relevant restrictions. Example: "International travel is **not** an allowable cost."]      |
| [Example: Senior & Other Personnel] | [$0]            | [Example: "No salaries for senior personnel, graduate/undergraduate students, or secretarial/clerical staff are approved."] |

---

### **Governing Regulations**

Compliance for this award is governed by a hierarchy of regulations. For any terms not specified directly in the award notice, the following documents apply:

* **Uniform Guidance (2 CFR 200):** [Note its role if mentioned]
* **NSF Research Terms and Conditions (RTCs):** [Include date, e.g., "Dated 11/12/2020"]
* **NSF Agency-Specific Requirements:** [Include date, e.g., "Dated 10/04/2021"]
* **NSF Proposal & Award Policies & Procedures Guide (PAPPG):** [Note any specific chapters mentioned]
* **NSF Solicitation:** [Include solicitation number/name]
* **Statutory Authority:** [Include act name, e.g., "National Science Foundation Act of 1950"]

---

### **Key Administrative & Reporting Requirements**

| Requirement Area       | Details                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| :--------------------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Financial & Progress Reporting** | [Summarize requirements. Note if deadlines are unspecified and refer to RTCs.]                                                                                                           |
| **Prior Approval** | [Summarize prior approval rules, referencing PAPPG if mentioned.]                                                                                                                                  |
| **Budget Modifications** | [State specific restrictions, especially regarding indirect cost rates.]                                                                                                                           |
| **Audit & Record-Keeping** | [Note that standard requirements from 2 CFR 200 apply, including the Single Audit threshold.]                                                                                                     |
| **Special Conditions** | [Summarize any unique conditions, such as PI transfer approval, noting the awardee's assumption of full responsibility.]                                                                             |

validation_plan.name: Header Fields Presence
validation_plan.description: Confirm that the final document begins with the **NSF Award Compliance & Financial Overview** heading followed by a bullet list containing *Project Title*, *Awardee*, *NSF Award Number (FAIN)*, and *Period of Performance* in the exact order and format shown in the template.

validation_plan.name: All Compliance Sections Included
validation_plan.description: Verify that the output contains each of the five required compliance sections—**STANDARD FEDERAL REQUIREMENTS**, **ADMINISTRATIVE TERMS & CONDITIONS**, **PROGRAMMATIC TERMS & CONDITIONS**, **HIGH-RISK OR SPECIAL CONDITIONS**, and **COMPLIANCE CALENDAR**—with their headings spelled exactly as shown. Each section must appear once, even if its content is only the phrase “Not specified in the document.”

validation_plan.name: Financial Summary Table Structure
validation_plan.description: Check that a markdown table titled **Financial Summary** is present, with two columns labeled “Financial Item” and “Details”. The table must include rows for at least: Total Award Amount, Budget Period Amounts (by year), Federal Share vs. Non‑Federal Share, Cost Sharing/Matching Requirements, Indirect Cost Rate and Base, and Period of Performance dates. Empty cells should contain the exact phrase “Not specified in the document”.

validation_plan.name: No Hallucinated or Modified Data
validation_plan.description: Inspect all numeric values, dates, percentages, regulation citations (e.g., “2 CFR 200”), and dollar amounts. They must match the phrasing and figures from the raw extraction steps. Any value that does not appear in the source extractions indicates a hallucination and fails this check.

validation_plan.name: Preserve Missing‑Info Notation
validation_plan.description: For any field where the extraction step explicitly reported “Not specified in the document”, ensure the same exact phrase appears in the final output at the appropriate location. The formatter must not replace it with guesses, blanks, or alternative wording such as “N/A”.

validation_plan.name: Markdown Formatting Consistency
validation_plan.description: Validate that the document uses proper Markdown: level‑2 headings (###) for each major section, bold text (**…**) for emphasized labels, horizontal rules (---) separating the overview from the financial and compliance parts, and correctly formatted tables with pipe delimiters and aligned columns. No raw JSON or plain‑text blocks should be present.

validation_plan.name: De‑duplication of Overlapping Facts
validation_plan.description: Cross‑check that any fact appearing in both the compliance extraction and the financial extraction (e.g., award period dates, total award amount) is presented only once in the merged summary. Duplicate entries of the same fact in separate sections constitute a failure.
